title: Kamlin
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Kamlin-KOTOR16.jpg]] |name=Kamlin |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3964 BBY]], [[Taris/Legends|Taris]][[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 1]] |species=[[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]]{{KOTORbackups|0}}''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]]While not explicitly stated in any text, Kamlin is depicted in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Commencement|''Commencement'']] story arc with long hair, female face, and [[Breast/Legends|breasts]]. This is most clearly seen in parts ''[[Knights of the Old Republic 1|1]]'' and ''[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|6]]'' of the arc. |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Dark]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Green |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] **[[Taris Four]]{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/fiction/f20080409/index.html|text="Labor Pains"|int=Labor Pains|newhost=Suvudu}} *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]][[Knights of the Old Republic 4|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 4]] |masters=[[Raana Tey]] |apprentices= }} {{Quote|In a rare interview, the leader of the Jedi on Taris, Master Lucien Draay, spoke highly of the new class… The other Padawans include Kamlin, the Falleen responsible for the capture of P'den Robalt…|Excerpt from "Taris Pins Hopes on New Jedi Class" in the [[Taris Holofeed|Taris Prime Edition Holofeed]]|}} '''Kamlin''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] training at the [[Jedi Tower|Jedi Temple]] on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] in the years following the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Trained initially on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] at the [[Jedi Enclave]], Kamlin was sent to Taris to study under [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Raana Tey]], who was also a member of the [[Jedi Covenant]] and [[First WatchCircle]], a group of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who sought to prevent the rise of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] at any cost. Kamlin excelled and showed great promise during her time on Taris and was credited with capturing the criminal [[P'den Robalt]]. During her final trial as a Padawan, she successfully navigated the "[[Rogue Moon]]," a [[Planet/Legends|planetoid]] in the [[Taris system/Legends|Taris system]] which [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] in contraflow through an [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]]. She survived the perpetual bombardment after being dropped onto the surface and, with no ability to see, was able to reach her master who waited in meditation at a high place. During their meditation, however, the Jedi Masters had a [[Rogue Moon Prophecy|vision]] about their [[Death/Legends|deaths]] which they supposed was due to the rise of a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. Through discussion, the masters connected their apprentices to the figure in the vision and decided to act. Aiming to prevent the rise of the Sith at any cost, they agreed to murder the Padawans and schemed to do so at their [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|knighting ceremony]]. Kamlin had earned the privilege to be knighted and attended the banquet in honor of the graduates. Following the abrupt and destructive entry of fellow Padawan [[Zayne Carrick]], she pitched in to help cover the damages to a large [[Window/Legends|window]] and tables. She then showed up at the ceremony on time and began discourse with her masters. Initially, she was excited that her friend, Carrick, was to be knighted as well. She, however, sensed something amiss due to the masters' strong disapproval of Carrick. Pressed, the Jedi Masters hastened their plans and [[Padawan Massacre|struck down]] the Padawans, pinning it on the late-arriving Carrick. Eventually, Carrick was cleared of Kamlin's murder. ==Biography== ===Jedi training=== Kamlin was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] who lived during the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] following the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Being found a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Kamlin was trained as part of a group of [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] at the [[Jedi Enclave]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. Other members of the group included [[Zayne Carrick]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]]; [[Gharn]], a [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]]; [[Shad Jelavan]], a Human; and [[Oojoh]], a [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]].[[Knights of the Old Republic 2|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 2]] These five trainees, having been accepted to study the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], grew up together as they were sent to a small [[Jedi Tower|training facility]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. There, each was assigned one of [[First WatchCircle|five]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] to continue their training. Kamlin was placed under the tutelage of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master [[Raana Tey]], a member of the [[Jedi Covenant]], which sought to stop the return of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] at any cost.''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' [[File:Rogue_moon_trial.jpg|thumb|left|200x200px|Kamlin, and the other Padawans being dropped onto the Rogue Moon for their trial]] Through her training, Kamlin became one of the most promising students of her class. For one training exercise, Kamlin captured the noted criminal [[P'den Robalt]], an accomplishment that was recognized by the Taris [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] feed. In [[3964 BBY]], Kamlin and the other four members of the next graduating class were taken to the place called the "[[Rogue Moon]]," a planetoid in the [[Taris system/Legends|Taris system]] that featured frequent and dangerous bombardment from [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]]. Dropped onto the surface in red [[Environment suit/Legends|environmental suits]], the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] were given the task to reach a high place where their masters meditated.[[Knights of the Old Republic 3|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 3]] Kamlin, along with the other Padawans, had no shield to protect her from the asteroid impacts, and the blast shield within the visor covered their eyes. Instead, the trainees were expected to rely entirely on the Force to reach their masters safely. While in meditation, the Jedi Masters, who as [[Jedi Consular|Consulars]] specialized in [[Precognition/Legends|precognition]], [[Rogue Moon Prophecy|shared a vision]] [[Force vision/Legends|through the Force]] of their [[Death/Legends|death]] along with the fall of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] by someone wearing red. Together, the Jedi Masters surmised that it was someone wearing a red environmental suit, such as those worn by their apprentices. Thus, the five believed it would be one of the Padawans who would fall to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and bring the threat of the Sith back to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. In response, the masters agreed that the issue needed to be dealt with.[[Knights of the Old Republic 5|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5]] Although she was not the first to finish the ascent, when Kamlin had reached her master, the Falleen waited inside the magnetic shield for Zayne Carrick, the last of the Padawans, to arrive. Returning to Taris, however, the masters sequestered themselves inside the temple for days. When they once again revealed themselves to their Padawans, it had been decided to arrange a banquet and [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|ceremony]] for the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|knighting]] of the five trainees. Even at the party for Kamlin and the other Padawans, the masters hastened the proceedings of the evening with the sudden and tardy arrival of Carrick. Having chased the criminal [[Marn Hierogryph]] through the Tarisian skylanes, Carrick crashed through a [[Window/Legends|window]] and into a table. He was instructed by his disapproving master, [[Lucien Draay]], to pay for the meal and the damages that had been incurred. Because of the expense, Kamlin and his other friends helped provide some of the [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] needed. Lucien Draay set one hour after his departure from the banquet to begin the knighting ceremony and instructed the Padawans to not be late. Kamlin arrived on time, along with Gharn and Oojoh, but Shad Jelavan and Zayne Carrick did not. Jelavan arrived thereafter and expressed surprise that Carrick was to be knighted with the others. While Jelavan was a friend to the other Human trainee, he sensed something was amiss, for he understood that Master Draay held Carrick in low regard and never took him seriously. Kamlin, too, then chimed in, expressing disbelief and questioning if everything was in order. Having been pressed, the masters hastily executed their plans.[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]] Raana Tey, along with the other Jedi Masters, ignited her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and [[Padawan Massacre|executed]] the Padawans, with Tey slicing Kamlin across the torso and right arm. ===Legacy=== {{Dialogue|Raana Tey|This— this is the temple on Taris. W— w— we abandoned it.|Kamlin|''We'' never left it.|Oojoh|And now you're back with us, Master Raana Tey.|attr=The [[Padawan Massacre|murdered Padawans]] appear in a Force vision to [[Raana Tey]]|src=[[Knights of the Old Republic 16|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 16]]}} [[File:XamarKillsGharn-KOTOR1.png|thumb|right|150px|Kamlin, laying [[Death/Legends|dead]] at the hands of [[Raana Tey]], her master]] Zayne Carrick arrived late to the knighting ceremony only to see his friends dead at the hands of their masters. He fled the scene and was framed for their murder. Carrick was then pursued by the Jedi and Republic officials for the crime. While being pursued, he prophesied that he would finally have his name cleared for the crimes which the masters committed. Shortly thereafter, the five Jedi Masters known as the First WatchCircle were reassigned to different positions apart from each other.[[Knights of the Old Republic 9|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 9]] Nevertheless, Raana Tey traveled back to Taris. While in transit, she had a nightmare in which she saw the Jedi Tower under siege. Running into the tower, she stumbled upon a knighting ceremony, as Zayne Carrick had. In the vision, however, she saw Kamlin standing in her place with a lightsaber drawn. Each of the other murdered apprentices were in the positions their masters had occupied during the massacre. Later, in [[3963 BBY]], the truth behind the covenant and the Padawan Massacre [[Vindication|came to light]] when Master [[Xamar]] testified before the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]. Xamar confessed to the plot and revealed the details about the Covenant, exonerating Carrick.[[Knights of the Old Republic 32|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 32]][[Knights of the Old Republic 35|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 35]] The Jedi never released the truth to the public, but Kamlin's parents were privately notified about the circumstances of their daughter's death.[[Knights of the Old Republic 34|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 34]] In the aftermath of the Vindication insurrection, Carrick brought together the family members of the murdered Padawans, including [[Kamlin's sister]]. Her sister became involved in the [[Rogue Moon Project]], an organization created by Carrick and Shel Jelavan to help clear other wrongfully accused individuals.[[Knights of the Old Republic 48|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 48]] Carrick further honored Kamlin with the usage of her name as part of his alias when infiltrating the [[Slavery/Legends|slave-trading]] organization known as "[[the Crucible]]."[[Knights of the Old Republic 45|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 45]] ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Us— and ''Zayne'', too! I never would have thought!"
"He's going to be so surprised!|[[Oojoh]] and Kamlin reacting with pleasure to the announcement that [[Zayne Carrick]] was to be [[Jedi Knight/Legends|knighted]]|}} Kamlin was a promising student of the Force who had been assigned to apprentice under Master Raana Tey. She achieved a noted level in her training, having captured the criminal P'den Robalt, which earned mention in the [[Taris Holofeed]]. Likewise, she successfully navigated the Rogue Moon to reach her masters, even though she was not the first to arrive and was expected to be knighted shortly thereafter. Kamlin was willing to act with generosity, as shown by her donation to Zayne Carrick to help pay for the damages he incurred at the banquet, and was genuinely pleased that Carrick was also "expected" to be knighted at the ceremony. Nevertheless, Kamlin knew of the masters's views on Carrick and spoke up to question why he was to be knighted. She was unable, however, to escape the surprise ambush by the Jedi Masters and was murdered by Raana Tey. During her final days on Taris, Kamlin wore a blue robe topped with a purple vest and orange trim. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:SWKOTOR1-FC.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Kamlin, held by [[Zayne Carrick]] following her death. While depicted on the cover, the scene never takes place within the comic.]] Kamlin was created by [[John Jackson Miller]] in the [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']]. Her first appearance was in the comic's [[2006]] debut, [[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 1]]. Kamlin later appeared in all of the other issues of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Commencement|''Commencement'']] story arc except for [[Knights of the Old Republic 5|issue 5]]. She was first identified by name, however, in the in-universe news bulletin ''[[The Taris Holofeed: Prime Edition]]'', which appeared in the back of [[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]]. Kamlin also made an appearance in this issue,[[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]] along with subsequent appearances in a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] in [[Knights of the Old Republic 9|issue 9]] and in a Force vision in [[Knights of the Old Republic 16|issue 16]]. The first [[canon]] confirmation of Kamlin as Tey's Padawan, however, occurred in the ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]''. Additionally, Kamlin appears on the cover of the first issue of the ''Knights of the Old Republic'' comic, being held by Zayne Carrick after her murder. This scene, however, never happens in the comic itself. She is also directly mentioned in the [[Knights of the Old Republic 48|48th issue]], where it is revealed Kamlin had a sister who was working for Carrick's newly-created Rogue Moon Project. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Kamlin}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]] *{{KOTORbackups|0}} {{1stm}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 2|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 2]] {{C|Corpse only}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 3|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 3]] {{Flash}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 4|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 4]] {{Flash}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 5|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5]] {{Flash}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]] {{Flash}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 9|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 9]] {{Hologram}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 16|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 16]] {{Vision|[[Raana Tey]]}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 23|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 23]] {{Imo}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 48|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 48]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Kamlin}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Kamlin |ka=კემლინი |pl=Kamlin }} [[Category:Falleen]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Jedi trainees of the Jedi Order]]
